one_line_thesis: West Pharmaceutical Services: global injectable packaging compounder with GLP-1 tailwind; watch at ~$354 on -1.1% synthesis IRR (~55× FY2025 owner cash).

deep_dive.executive_summary: West Pharmaceutical Services is a global injectable packaging compounder with FY2025 operating profit of $584.9 million (19.0% consolidated margin) on $3.07 billion of sales. Owner cash per share is about $6.45 (FY2025 cash from operations minus capital spending). Q1 2026 net sales were $844.9 million (+21% year over year) with diluted earnings per share of $1.92 and $297.6 million of share repurchases. At $353.68 (July 2026), base expected annual return is -1.1% per year, below our 15% hold band. Stable moat on validated elastomer components and customer qualification; partial dhando (net ...

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

valuation_decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

insight_events.title: Operating margin accelerating
insight_events.summary: Operating margin growth moved from -3.5% to +36.7% YoY for two consecutive quarters. Trailing-twelve-month growth confirms the same direction. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.
insight_events.why_it_matters: Positive evidence. May change earnings power, cash generation, or the valuation range.
insight_events.recommended_follow_up: Compare this development with the thesis, falsifiers, and valuation assumptions.

timeline.label: Completed sale and transfer of SmartDose 3.5mL on-body delivery system manufacturing and supply rights
Michel Lagarde appointed President and CEO effective June 1, 2026, succeeding retiring Eric M. Green
Q1 2026: net sales $844.9M (+21% YoY); diluted EPS $1.92; $297.6M share repurchases
Announced Dublin facility expansion to support high-volume injectable therapies including diabetes and obesity
Eric M. Green informed Board of plans to retire as President, CEO, and Chair
FY2025: net sales $3.07B (+6.2%); operating income $584.9M; owner cash ~$6.45/sh; cash $791.3M
December 2025 restructuring plan initiated; asset-related charges alongside January 2025 plan
FY2024: net sales $2.89B; repurchased $560.9M of stock; owner cash growth moderated after 2023 destocking
January 2025 restructuring plan; elastomer asset impairments and EP&C investment focus
Sold one Proprietary Products manufacturing facility; gain recognized in other income
Accelerated investment in self-injection systems, integrated drug containment, and high-value components
Renamed from West Co Inc to West Pharmaceutical Services; focus on injectable containment components

industry.structure: Oligopoly in primary injectable packaging: West, Datwyler, and Aptar dominate elastomer stoppers, seals, and drug-delivery devices; high regulatory validation and customer qualification create switching costs.
industry.share_shift: GLP-1 and biologics injectable volumes favor suppliers with scale in high-quality elastomers and fill-finish capacity; West gaining share on component demand while contract manufacturing competes with Phillips Medisize and regional assemblers.
industry.trend: Injectable obesity and diabetes therapies drive component volumes; oral GLP-1 competition is the primary format risk; customer inventory cycles and elastomer raw-material costs remain swing factors.

top_claims.claim: Leadership / governance on watch. 2 executive sales ($1,129,153) in 120d

West Pharmaceutical Services is a global injectable packaging compounder with FY2025 operating profit of $584.9 million (19.0% consolidated margin) on $3.07 billion of sales. Owner cash per share is about $6.45 (FY2025 cash from operations minus capital spending). Q1 2026 net sales were $844.9 million (+21% year over y

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Oral GLP-1 adoption and customer inventory destocking reduce injectable component orders faster than capacity additions convert to cash.

Inversion (Munger): What kills the thesis? Major pharma customers shift obesity share to oral pills; Proprietary Products organic growth falls below 3% for four quarters; elastomer impairments repeat.
CEO transition: Eric M. Green retiring; Michel Lagarde became CEO June 2026. Track record at scale is [HUMAN REVIEW].
